The Washington Water Power
company barn trouble is productive
of more trouble among the unions
interested, two federal organiza-
tons being now involved besides
the carpenters. Federal union No.
11,624, comprising shovellers, 1t Ia
claimed, put men to work after
Union No. 1922 had pulled it's men
off in compliance with orders of the
Structural trades.


The attention of the Central Las
Dor union was called to the matter
and the question was left to the
executive committee with power to
act. Nothing definite has been
done yet, but it Is said No. 1924
will be shut out of the central body
unless it leaves the work 1t took
from the other union.
